description: buddyburn v1 — an app that motivates buddies to burn calories by letting users remotely check in together when they start a workout, tracking group workouts and streaks with Burn Buddies and Burn Squads.

userStories.title: Initialize monorepo with Next.js web app and API service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffold so that all subsequent work has a consistent foundation. Set up a TypeScript monorepo with a Next.js web app (apps/web), an API service (services/api), and a shared types/utilities package (packages/shared). Configure TypeScript strict mode and pino for structured logging in the API service.
acceptanceCriteria: Monorepo workspace is configured (yarn workspaces or npm workspaces)
apps/web is a working Next.js 14+ app with TypeScript strict mode
services/api is a Node.js 24 service with TypeScript strict mode
packages/shared exports shared TypeScript types and is referenced by both apps
pino is installed in services/api with JSON log output on startup
yarn build (or equivalent) succeeds in all packages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Monorepo scaffold created with yarn workspaces, Next.js 14 web app, Express API service with pino, and shared types package.

userStories.title: Initialize Expo React Native mobile app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Expo mobile app scaffold so that mobile screens can be built later. Create apps/mobile as an Expo (React Native) app with TypeScript strict mode, referencing packages/shared for shared types. Target iOS and Android.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/mobile is a valid Expo SDK 51+ project with TypeScript
packages/shared types are importable from apps/mobile
expo start --web runs without errors (basic sanity check)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Expo SDK 55 app scaffolded in apps/mobile with TypeScript strict mode, @burnbuddy/shared types imported, metro.config.js configured for monorepo, expo export --platform web succeeds.

userStories.title: Docker and docker-compose for local development
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run all services locally with a single command so that onboarding is fast. Create a Dockerfile for services/api and a docker-compose.yml that starts the API service and (optionally) Firebase Local Emulator Suite.
acceptanceCriteria: services/api has a Dockerfile that builds and starts the Node.js service
docker-compose.yml in project root starts services/api
docker compose up starts the API service and it logs a startup message via pino
docker compose up does not require any secrets to be present (gracefully starts in dev mode without Firebase)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Multi-stage Dockerfile in services/api/ builds from monorepo root context (skips mobile/web deps). docker-compose.yml at project root starts the API on port 3001. .dockerignore excludes node_modules, dist, and env files. No secrets required — service starts gracefully without Firebase. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: OpenTelemetry and pino-opentelemetry-transport setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need structured logging and tracing wired up so that observability works from the start. Configure pino with pino-opentelemetry-transport in services/api and the Next.js API routes in apps/web. In apps/mobile, add a lightweight console wrapper that matches the pino interface.
acceptanceCriteria: pino and pino-opentelemetry-transport are installed in services/api
OpenTelemetry SDK is initialized in services/api on startup (traces exported to stdout in dev)
apps/web API routes use pino for structured logging
packages/shared exports a logger interface with a mobile stub that wraps console
A sample log line in services/api emits valid JSON with level, msg, and time fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OTel SDK (ConsoleSpanExporter, traces to stdout in dev) initialized in services/api/src/instrumentation.ts. pino-opentelemetry-transport installed and wired as an optional transport target (enabled via OTEL_LOGS_ENABLED=true env var for production). apps/web has pino logger at src/lib/logger.ts and uses it in the health API route. packages/shared exports Logger interface and createMobileLogger() console stub. All typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Firebase Auth backend middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all API endpoints protected by Firebase Auth tokens so that only authenticated users can call the API. Add an Express/Next.js middleware that validates the Firebase Auth JWT from the Authorization header. Use Firebase Local Emulator Suite in tests so no live Firebase project is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: firebase-admin is installed in services/api
A middleware function verifies the Firebase Auth token from the Authorization: Bearer <token> header
Requests with a missing or invalid token receive a 401 response
Requests with a valid token have the decoded user uid attached to the request context
Integration tests use the Firebase Auth Local Emulator (FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST set in test env)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: firebase-admin installed in services/api. src/lib/firebase.ts initializes Admin SDK (emulator-aware via FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST). src/middleware/auth.ts validates Bearer token, returns 401 on missing/invalid, attaches { uid } to req.user. Express Request type augmented in src/types/express.d.ts. vitest + supertest added; 4 unit tests all pass. All workspace typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Firestore user profile schema and API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a user profile document in Firestore so that user data (display name, email, FCM token) can be stored and retrieved. Define the UserProfile TypeScript type in packages/shared, create the Firestore collection, and add GET/PUT /users/me API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: UserProfile type is defined in packages/shared: { uid, email, displayName, fcmToken?, createdAt }
Firestore collection users/<uid> stores UserProfile documents
GET /users/me returns the authenticated user's profile (or 404 if not yet created)
PUT /users/me upserts the authenticated user's profile
POST /users (called after signup) creates the user profile document
Unit tests use the Firestore Local Emulator
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UserProfile type was already in packages/shared. Firestore singleton in services/api/src/lib/firestore.ts. Users router (POST /users, GET /users/me, PUT /users/me) in services/api/src/routes/users.ts. 13 vitest tests using vi.mock for Firestore (Firebase emulator unavailable in devcontainer). All tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Firestore friends schema and API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add friends so that I can later create Burn Buddies and Burn Squads with them. Define Friend and FriendRequest Firestore types and implement the friends API: search by email, send request, accept request, delete friend.
acceptanceCriteria: FriendRequest type defined in packages/shared: { id, fromUid, toUid, status: 'pending'|'accepted'|'declined', createdAt }
GET /users/search?email=... returns matching user (uid, displayName, email) or 404
POST /friends/requests sends a friend request to another user by uid
GET /friends/requests returns pending incoming and outgoing friend requests for the authenticated user
POST /friends/requests/:id/accept accepts a friend request; creates a bidirectional friendship document
DELETE /friends/:uid removes a friendship without requiring the other user's approval
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Friend type added to packages/shared. FriendRequest type was already present. GET /users/search added to users router (Firestore where query by email). New friends router (friends.ts) implements POST /friends/requests, GET /friends/requests, POST /friends/requests/:id/accept, DELETE /friends/:uid. Friendship stored as friends/{uid1}_{uid2} (sorted). 20 vitest tests using vi.mock with per-collection mock chains. All 37 tests and typechecks pass. Note: @types/express v5 types req.params values as string|string[], fixed with 'as string' casts.

userStories.title: Firestore Burn Buddy schema and API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send and accept Burn Buddy requests so that I have a bilateral one-on-one workout partner. A Burn Buddy relationship is distinct from a Burn Squad — no admin role, no group settings.
acceptanceCriteria: BurnBuddy type defined in packages/shared: { id, uid1, uid2, workoutSchedule?, createdAt }
BurnBuddyRequest type: { id, fromUid, toUid, status: 'pending'|'accepted'|'declined', createdAt }
POST /burn-buddies/requests sends a Burn Buddy request to a friend (must already be friends)
GET /burn-buddies/requests returns pending incoming and outgoing Burn Buddy requests
POST /burn-buddies/requests/:id/accept accepts a request; creates the BurnBuddy document for both users
GET /burn-buddies returns all accepted Burn Buddies for the authenticated user
DELETE /burn-buddies/:id removes a Burn Buddy relationship
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BurnBuddy and BurnBuddyRequest types were already in packages/shared. New burn-buddies router (services/api/src/routes/burn-buddies.ts) implements POST /burn-buddies/requests (friendship check required), GET /burn-buddies/requests, POST /burn-buddies/requests/:id/accept (creates BurnBuddy doc with random UUID), GET /burn-buddies (two parallel queries: uid1==uid + uid2==uid), DELETE /burn-buddies/:id (member-only auth check). 21 vitest tests using vi.mock with separate mock chains per collection. All 58 tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Firestore Burn Squad schema and API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create and manage Burn Squads (groups) with my friends so that we can work out together. The creator is the admin. Groups are always called Burn Squads regardless of member count.
acceptanceCriteria: BurnSquad type defined in packages/shared: { id, name, adminUid, memberUids, settings: { onlyAdminsCanAddMembers: boolean, workoutSchedule?: WorkoutSchedule }, createdAt }
POST /burn-squads creates a new Burn Squad; creator becomes admin; sends join requests to specified friend uids
GET /burn-squads returns all Burn Squads the authenticated user is a member of
POST /burn-squads/:id/join-requests/:requestId/accept accepts a squad join request
POST /burn-squads/:id/members adds a friend to the squad (respects onlyAdminsCanAddMembers setting)
PUT /burn-squads/:id/settings updates squad settings (admin only)
DELETE /burn-squads/:id deletes the squad (admin only)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BurnSquadJoinRequest type added to packages/shared. New burn-squads router (services/api/src/routes/burn-squads.ts) implements POST /burn-squads (creates squad, sends join requests to friends), GET /burn-squads (array-contains query on memberUids), POST /burn-squads/:id/join-requests/:requestId/accept (read-then-write memberUids update), POST /burn-squads/:id/members (friendship check + onlyAdminsCanAddMembers gate, sends join request), PUT /burn-squads/:id/settings (admin only), DELETE /burn-squads/:id (admin only). 30 vitest tests using vi.mock with separate mock chains per collection. All 88 tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Firestore workout schema and workout API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start, specify the type of, and end a workout so that my activity is recorded. Workout types come from a predefined list but can also be custom.
acceptanceCriteria: Workout type defined in packages/shared: { id, uid, type: WorkoutType | string, startedAt, endedAt?, status: 'active'|'completed' }
WorkoutType enum in packages/shared: 'Weightlifting' | 'Running' | 'Cycling' | 'Yoga' | 'Barre' | 'Swimming' | 'HIIT' | 'Custom'
POST /workouts starts a new workout for the authenticated user (status: active)
PATCH /workouts/:id/end ends the active workout (sets endedAt, status: completed)
A scheduled job or Cloud Function auto-ends workouts still active after 1.5 hours
GET /workouts returns the authenticated user's workout history
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Workout and WorkoutType types were already in packages/shared. New workouts router (services/api/src/routes/workouts.ts) implements POST /workouts (status: active), PATCH /workouts/:id/end (sets endedAt + status: completed), GET /workouts (history by uid). Exported autoEndStaleWorkouts() function queries workouts where status==active AND startedAt<=cutoff (ISO string comparison). setInterval in index.ts calls it every 10 minutes for local dev; can also be called by a Cloud Function in production. 16 vitest tests using vi.mock. All 104 tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Group workout detection service
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Group Workout to be automatically recorded when my Burn Buddy or all members of a Burn Squad start working out within 20 minutes of each other so that our shared effort is tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupWorkout type defined in packages/shared: { id, type: 'buddy'|'squad', referenceId (burnBuddyId or burnSquadId), memberUids, startedAt, workoutIds }
When a workout is started, the service checks all of the user's Burn Buddies — if the buddy also has an active workout started within 20 min, a GroupWorkout is recorded
When a workout is started, the service checks all of the user's Burn Squads — if ALL other members also have active workouts started within 20 min, a GroupWorkout is recorded
Each qualifying group triggers exactly one GroupWorkout document (not duplicate on the buddy's end)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New detectGroupWorkouts() service in services/api/src/services/group-workout-detection.ts. Checks all BurnBuddies (two parallel queries: uid1==uid + uid2==uid) — if partner has an active workout started within 20 min, creates GroupWorkout (type: buddy). Checks all BurnSquads (array-contains query) — if ALL other members have active workouts within 20 min, creates GroupWorkout (type: squad). Deduplication: before creating, queries groupWorkouts collection for existing doc with same referenceId and startedAt >= cutoff. Called fire-and-forget from POST /workouts handler after saving workout. 14 vitest tests. All 118 tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Burn Streak and Supernova Streak calculation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my Burn Streak and Supernova Streak for each Burn Buddy and Burn Squad so that I can track sustained commitment.
acceptanceCriteria: BurnStreak calculation: count the number of consecutive calendar days on which ANY member of the buddy/squad completed a workout
SupernovaStreak calculation: count the number of consecutive calendar days on which ALL members of the buddy/squad completed a workout
GET /burn-buddies/:id/streaks returns { burnStreak: number, supernovaStreak: number } for the given Burn Buddy
GET /burn-squads/:id/streaks returns { burnStreak: number, supernovaStreak: number } for the given Burn Squad
Streak resets to 0 if the qualifying condition is not met for any calendar day
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New calculateStreaks() pure function in services/api/src/services/streak-calculator.ts. Groups completed workouts by UTC calendar date, walks backwards from today counting consecutive qualifying days. BurnStreak counts any-member days; SupernovaStreak counts all-member days. GET /burn-buddies/:id/streaks and GET /burn-squads/:id/streaks added to respective routers, both protected by requireAuth + membership check. 11 unit tests for the calculator + 5 route tests each (401, 404, 403, zero workouts, with workouts). All 139 tests and typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Push notification service
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive a push notification when my Burn Buddy or a Burn Squad member starts a workout so that I can join within the 20-minute window.
acceptanceCriteria: firebase-admin FCM integration is set up in services/api
When a workout is started, notifications are sent to all of the user's Burn Buddies (via their stored fcmToken)
When a workout is started, notifications are sent to all other members of each of the user's Burn Squads (via their stored fcmTokens)
Notification payload includes: title (e.g. 'Rahul started a workout!'), body ('Jump in within 20 min to log a group workout'), and a data payload with { type: 'WORKOUT_STARTED', uid }
Users with no fcmToken stored are skipped silently
Tests pass (use FCM emulator or mock the FCM send call)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: New sendWorkoutStartedNotifications(uid, senderDisplayName) service in services/api/src/services/push-notifications.ts. Queries burnBuddies (two parallel uid1/uid2 queries) and burnSquads (array-contains) to collect recipient UIDs (deduplicated via Set). Fetches UserProfile for each recipient, skips those with no fcmToken. Calls admin.messaging().sendEachForMulticast() with title '{name} started a workout!', body 'Jump in within 20 min to log a group workout', data { type: WORKOUT_STARTED, uid }. Called fire-and-forget from POST /workouts after fetching sender's displayName from Firestore. 9 unit tests, 148 total tests pass. All typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web authentication UI — login and signup
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create an account and log in via the web app so that I can access buddyburn. Support email/password and Google OAuth via Firebase Authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page at /login with email/password fields and a 'Sign in with Google' button
Sign-up page at /signup with email/password fields and a 'Sign up with Google' button
Firebase Auth SDK (client-side) handles email/password and Google OAuth sign-in
On successful login, the Firebase Auth token is stored and attached to subsequent API calls
Unauthenticated users are redirected to /login when accessing protected routes
Authenticated users visiting /login are redirected to /
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: firebase v12 installed in apps/web. src/lib/firebase-client.ts initializes Firebase app (emulator-aware via NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST). src/lib/auth-context.tsx provides AuthProvider with onAuthStateChanged; sets auth_session cookie for middleware route guards. src/lib/api.ts provides typed fetch helpers that attach Firebase ID token as Bearer header. /login and /signup pages handle email/password + Google OAuth via signInWithEmailAndPassword/createUserWithEmailAndPassword/signInWithPopup. src/middleware.ts guards all routes using auth_session cookie; redirects / → /login when unauthenticated and /login → / when already authenticated. next.config.ts renamed to next.config.mjs (Next.js 14 does not support .ts config). Browser verified: redirect and all form fields confirmed.

userStories.title: Web onboarding — Getting Started card
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to see a Getting Started card that explains how to add my first friend so that I know where to begin.
acceptanceCriteria: New users see a 'Getting Started' card on the Burn Buddies home page describing how to add a friend
The card has an 'x' button that dismisses it and saves the dismissed state (so it does not reappear on refresh or revisit)
Dismissed state is persisted in the user's Firestore profile document
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GettingStartedCard component in apps/web/src/components/GettingStartedCard.tsx with 'x' dismiss button. page.tsx fetches GET /users/me on mount; shows card when !profile.gettingStartedDismissed (or shows by default on 404 for new users). Dismiss handler calls PUT /users/me with gettingStartedDismissed:true. gettingStartedDismissed field added to UserProfile type in packages/shared. PUT /users/me updated to handle gettingStartedDismissed field. New test for gettingStartedDismissed update (149 total tests pass). All typechecks pass. Browser verified: login page renders, middleware redirect confirmed.

userStories.title: Web friends page UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search for other users by email and manage my friends list on the web so that I can connect with people to burn calories with.
acceptanceCriteria: Friends page at /friends shows the accepted friends list
'Add Friend' button opens a search input for email lookup
Typing an email and selecting a result shows a confirmation dialog before sending the friend request
Pending outgoing and incoming requests are shown at the top of the page
Incoming requests have 'Accept' and 'Ignore' actions (Ignore deferred to v2 — button is present but no-ops for now)
Accepted friends can be removed via a 'Remove' action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GET /friends added to API (enriched friend list with displayName/email via two parallel uid1/uid2 Firestore queries + user profile lookups). GET /users/:uid added for public profile lookup by uid. /friends page at apps/web/src/app/friends/page.tsx: Add Friend button opens email search panel, search result click opens confirmation dialog, POST /friends/requests on confirm. Pending incoming requests shown with Accept + Ignore (no-op) buttons. Outgoing requests shown with pending badge. Accepted friends listed with Remove button (DELETE /friends/:uid). 157 tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web Burn Buddies home page UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my Burn Buddies and Burn Squads on a single home page, ordered by most recent group workout, so that I have an at-a-glance view of my workout relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page at / (or /burn-buddies) lists all Burn Buddies and Burn Squads in a combined list
Each list item shows: partner/squad name, Burn Streak count, time since last group workout
List is ordered by most recent group workout (most recent at top); items with no group workouts appear at the bottom
Tapping a list item navigates to the Burn Buddy or Burn Squad detail page
Buttons to create a new Burn Buddy and a new Burn Squad are present
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: New GET /group-workouts API endpoint (services/api/src/routes/group-workouts.ts) queries groupWorkouts collection by memberUids array-contains. Home page (apps/web/src/app/page.tsx) overhauled: fetches burn buddies, burn squads, group workouts in parallel; enriches each buddy with partner display name (GET /users/:uid) and streak (GET /burn-buddies/:id/streaks); enriches each squad with streak (GET /burn-squads/:id/streaks); sorts combined list by most recent group workout desc (items without group workouts at bottom); links to /burn-buddies/:id and /burn-squads/:id detail pages. '+ Burn Buddy' and '+ Burn Squad' create buttons added. Nav bar with Friends link and Sign out. GettingStartedCard preserved. 160 tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web create Burn Buddy flow UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send a Burn Buddy request to a friend from the web app so that we become workout partners.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Burn Buddy sub-page at /burn-buddies/new (or a modal)
User can select a friend from their friends list
Submitting sends the Burn Buddy request via the API
Pending incoming Burn Buddy requests are shown at the top of the Burn Buddies home page with 'Accept' action
Accepting a request updates the home page list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: /burn-buddies/new page (apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/new/page.tsx) lists friends, select toggles highlight+checkmark, shows confirmation panel, POST /burn-buddies/requests on confirm, redirects to /. Home page (page.tsx) refactored: loadData() extracted to component-level useCallback so accept handler can call it. Pending incoming burn buddy requests shown above the list with Accept button (POST /burn-buddies/requests/:id/accept + reload). 160 tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web Burn Buddy detail page UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view detailed stats for a specific Burn Buddy so that I can see our shared progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Burn Buddy detail page at /burn-buddies/:id
Shows: partner's name, number of group workouts this week and this month, Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, how long the Burn Buddy has existed
Full workout log for workouts associated with this Burn Buddy is shown in reverse chronological order
Edit button allows updating the workout schedule
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GET /burn-buddies/:id and PUT /burn-buddies/:id endpoints added to API. apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx created: fetches burn buddy, partner profile, streaks, and group workouts in parallel; displays partner name, Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, group workouts this week/month, buddy age, and formatted creation date; shows full group workout log in reverse chrono order; Edit Schedule button opens an inline form with day toggles and optional time input; saves via PUT /burn-buddies/:id. 168 tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web create Burn Squad flow UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a Burn Squad with multiple friends on the web app so that we can work out as a group.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Burn Squad sub-page at /burn-squads/new
User enters a squad name
User selects one or more friends from their friends list to invite
User sets an optional workout schedule (days of week + time)
Submitting calls the POST /burn-squads API; invitees receive join requests
Pending incoming squad join requests are shown at the top of the Burn Buddies home page with 'Accept' action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: /burn-squads/new page (apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/new/page.tsx): squad name input (required), multi-select friend list with toggle highlight+checkmark, day-of-week schedule toggle buttons, optional time input (shown when days selected), submit calls POST /burn-squads with { name, inviteUids, workoutSchedule? }. POST /burn-squads API updated to accept optional workoutSchedule in body and store it in settings.workoutSchedule. GET /burn-squads/join-requests endpoint added (returns pending incoming + outgoing enriched with squad names). Home page updated: fetches squad join requests, shows pending incoming invitations at top with blue Accept button (POST /burn-squads/:id/join-requests/:requestId/accept + reload). 4 new tests for GET /join-requests. 172 total tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web Burn Squad detail page UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view detailed stats and manage my Burn Squad on the web so that I can track group progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Burn Squad detail page at /burn-squads/:id
Shows: squad name, member list (with avatars/names), group workouts this week and this month, Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, how long the squad has existed
Full group workout log for this squad in reverse chronological order
Admin can edit squad settings (name, member permissions, workout schedule)
Admin can delete the squad with a confirmation dialog
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GET /burn-squads/:id endpoint added (member-only, returns single squad). PUT /burn-squads/:id endpoint added (admin-only, updates name and/or settings, returns updated squad). apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/[id]/page.tsx created: fetches squad, member profiles (GET /users/:uid for each), streaks, and group workouts in parallel; displays squad name + Squad badge, 6-stat grid (Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, this week/month group workouts, squad age, created date), member list with avatar initials + admin badge, full group workout log in reverse chrono order. Admin-only Edit Settings panel: squad name input, onlyAdminsCanAddMembers checkbox, day-of-week schedule toggles, optional time input. Admin-only Delete Squad button with confirmation dialog (routes to / on success). 9 new API tests (4 GET + 5 PUT), 181 total tests pass, all typechecks pass.

userStories.title: Web start workout UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start a workout from the web app, specify what type of workout I'm doing, and end it when I'm done so that my activity is recorded and my buddies are notified.
acceptanceCriteria: A prominent 'Start Workout' button is accessible from the home page
Tapping opens a workout type selector with the predefined list (Weightlifting, Running, Cycling, Yoga, Barre, Swimming, HIIT, Custom) and a text input for Custom type
Confirming calls POST /workouts and triggers push notifications to buddies/squad members
While a workout is active, the UI shows an active workout banner with an 'End Workout' button and elapsed time
Tapping 'End Workout' calls PATCH /workouts/:id/end
If the workout auto-ends after 1.5h, the active banner disappears on next page load
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Start Workout button on home page opens a workout type selector modal with 8 types (Weightlifting, Running, Cycling, Yoga, Barre, Swimming, HIIT, Custom). Custom type shows a text input. Confirming calls POST /workouts; push notifications to buddies/squad members are triggered server-side. Active workout banner shows elapsed time (live timer via setInterval) and End Workout button (calls PATCH /workouts/:id/end). On next page load, GET /workouts is fetched to check for any active workout — if the workout auto-ended after 1.5h, no active banner appears. All typechecks pass, 181 tests pass.

userStories.title: Web account management UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want an account management page on the web where I can update my profile and toggle the Getting Started card.
acceptanceCriteria: Account page at /account
Shows current display name and email
Toggle to re-enable the Getting Started card (sets the dismissed flag back to false in Firestore)
Log out button signs out the user via Firebase Auth and redirects to /login
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Mobile auth and onboarding screens (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to create an account and log in via the Expo app so that I can use buddyburn on iOS or Android.
acceptanceCriteria: Login screen with email/password fields and 'Sign in with Google' button (using expo-auth-session or @react-native-google-signin)
Sign-up screen with email/password fields
Firebase Auth SDK (react-native-firebase or firebase/app) handles auth
On successful login, Firebase Auth token is attached to all API calls
Unauthenticated users are shown the login screen
Getting Started card on home screen for new users, dismissible with preference persisted to Firestore
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile friends screen (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to search for friends and manage friend requests on my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Friends tab/screen shows accepted friends list
Add Friend button opens email search
Selecting a search result shows a confirmation before sending the friend request
Pending incoming and outgoing friend requests shown at top with Accept action for incoming
Friends can be removed via swipe or menu action
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile Burn Buddies home and create flow (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to see my Burn Buddies home page and create new Burn Buddies from my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen lists Burn Buddies and Burn Squads with streak counts and time since last group workout, ordered by most recent group workout
Pending Burn Buddy and squad join requests shown at top with Accept action
Create Burn Buddy flow: select a friend, send request
Tapping a Burn Buddy navigates to the detail screen showing group workout count (week/month), Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, how long it has existed, and workout log
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile Burn Squads screens (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to create and view Burn Squads from my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Burn Squad flow: enter name, select friends, set optional schedule, submit
Burn Squad detail screen: member list, group workout count (week/month), Burn Streak, Supernova Streak, workout log
Admin can access squad settings (member permissions, schedule, delete squad) from detail screen
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile start workout screen (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to start and end a workout from my phone so that my activity is recorded and my buddies are notified.
acceptanceCriteria: Prominent 'Start Workout' button on the home screen
Workout type selector with predefined list and custom text input
Confirming calls POST /workouts via the API
Active workout banner with elapsed time and 'End Workout' button while workout is in progress
Ending the workout calls PATCH /workouts/:id/end
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile push notification registration and handling (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to receive push notifications when my Burn Buddy or a Burn Squad member starts a workout so that I know to join in.
acceptanceCriteria: App requests push notification permission on first launch using expo-notifications
On permission granted, the Expo push token (or FCM token) is saved to the user's Firestore profile via PUT /users/me
App handles foreground notifications by showing an in-app alert/banner
App handles background/quit notifications via the system notification tray
Tapping the notification opens the app to the relevant Burn Buddy or Burn Squad
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile account management screen (Expo)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to manage my account and toggle the Getting Started card from my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: Account tab/screen shows display name and email
Toggle to re-enable the Getting Started card
Log out button signs out via Firebase Auth and returns to login screen
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Azure Container Apps Bicep templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Bicep IaC templates to provision the buddyburn infrastructure in Azure so that deployments are repeatable and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Bicep templates in infra/ provision a new resource group buddyburn-prod with an Azure Container Apps environment
Container App for services/api is defined with environment variable references to Azure Key Vault secrets
Container App for apps/web (Next.js) is defined
Azure Key Vault reference is declared (Key Vault must already exist; templates reference it, not create it)
A buddyburn-dev resource group variant is defined for the dev environment
az deployment group create --template-file infra/main.bicep dry run (--what-if) completes without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want GitHub Actions to build, test, and deploy the app to Azure Container Apps on push to main so that production deployments are automated.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy.yml triggers on push to main
Pipeline runs typecheck and tests for all packages
Pipeline builds Docker images for services/api and apps/web and pushes to Azure Container Registry
Pipeline deploys to Azure Container Apps using az containerapp update
Secrets are read from Azure Key Vault via a service principal (AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID stored as GitHub Actions secrets — documented in README, not committed)
A separate workflow or make target supports local production deploy (az commands wrapped in a script)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Firebase and Azure test emulator setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a documented, reproducible test environment using local emulators so that no live cloud services are required to run the test suite.
acceptanceCriteria: firebase.json configures Auth and Firestore emulators with fixed ports
A test helper in packages/shared initializes firebase-admin against the local emulators when FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST and FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST are set
All existing service tests (friends, burn buddy, burn squad, workout, streaks) run against the local emulators
A local .env.test documents all required env vars for running tests (no real secrets needed)
README section explains how to start the emulators and run tests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
